# On Providence

*[Seneca](../philosophers/seneca.md) · 64*

A short dialogue in which Seneca asks why misfortunes strike good people if the world is governed by [providence](../glossary/providence.md). His Stoic answer: adversity is a trial through which the divine tests and strengthens virtuous souls, as a stern father toughens his sons.
